title: Orchestrator Prompt — Phase 1: Definition Factory
what_it_is: The pasteable operating prompt for the Definition Factory sub-orchestrator: it directs a fresh /effort ultracode session to fan out (never do work inline) and produce a certified Service Definition Pack (PRD-delta, frozen+tagged contract, test plan, Service Done Definition, per-agent H1-H17 build prompt) for each of the 16 family services across 4 dependency-ordered waves (contracts/lib seam -> staging/workgraph -> 11 delta-packs -> UI surface-waves). It is explicitly labeled Phase 1 of the 4-phase program plan 5eFdxN3edd, preceded by Phase 0 and followed by the Phase 2 prompt (Ng66su4dVG), and it reuses (by citation, not duplication) the Act-3 Delivery Orchestrator (gkkUDzn277), the Foundation Orchestrator (UKanXYLCQD), the Issue Authoring Prompt (9VUHxAcoXw), and the SE-Arch review agent (8sYi523i4t).
overlaps: No conflicting duplicate found. It correctly nests under the live plan-of-record 5eFdxN3edd (cites it as sole source of truth, invents nothing beyond it) and hands off cleanly to the Phase-2 prompt Ng66su4dVG via the contract-tag dispatch gate defined in its own Exit Criteria (Phase 2 dispatches only against tagged contracts this phase produces). It cites, rather than restates, the reusable machinery (gkkUDzn277 Delivery Orchestrator, UKanXYLCQD Foundation Orchestrator, 9VUHxAcoXw Issue Authoring, 8sYi523i4t SE-Arch) -- proper reuse, not duplication. The new parallel-build-plan.md (8-teams-by-service, contract-tag unlock) is scoped to BUILD execution against already-tagged contracts, which is Phase-2 territory (isolated builds), not Phase-1 (contract definition/tagging); it does not belong folded into this page. Confusion risk is low but real if someone mistakenly merges the new plan's 8-team build structure into this page's Wave 1-4 pack-authoring structure -- the two describe adjacent but distinct phases (define-and-freeze vs. build-against-frozen) and must stay separated at Ng66su4dVG instead.
